registry_meta.title: Seed control-plane artifact registry
registry_meta.status_note: artifact_status is independent from master-plan task status markers

validation_hook_standards.evidence_required: Required fields present for every artifact or artifact type record
Referenced artifact IDs and edge fields resolve
Source-authority references and material grounding are explicit
Terminology, dependencies, and boundaries are consistent with accepted upstream artifacts
Explicit human acceptance for downstream use
Revalidation required after validator or upstream acceptance change
Only used when real fixtures, harnesses, or test contracts exist

stale_rule_catalog.rule: Accepted downstream artifacts connected by hard dependency edges become stale on accepted source-authority change; reference-only links do not propagate by default.
Hard dependencies auto-stale on accepted upstream change; validator changes require revalidation.
Accepted upstream changes trigger stale review by default; deterministic derivations may opt into auto propagation per dependency edge.
Sync and delta artifacts summarize accepted truth and normally require human stale review on upstream change.
Reference-only edges do not propagate stale status by default.

artifact_types.purpose: External or mirrored authority document governing platform meaning

artifact_types.name: Execution packet

artifact_types.name: Delta, sync, or stale-detection pack
artifact_types.purpose: Delta capture, recurring sync, stale rules, or gate re-check artifact

artifacts.title: Repo / Agent Operating Contract

dependency_refs.notes: P0.2 scope, deliverables, and acceptance rules come from the master plan.
The operating contract reuses accepted P0.1 artifact, validation, and stale semantics.
The operating contract preserves the accepted three-layer architecture.
The operating contract preserves the fixed release doctrine and forbidden shortcuts.
The operating contract preserves the north-star commissioned-work direction.

notes: P0.2 normative human-readable operating contract.
Defines the accepted-artifact-only default, AGENTS projection rules, and packet-brief baseline.

artifacts.title: Repo root AGENTS projection

dependency_refs.notes: Root AGENTS is the concise repo-wide projection of the accepted operating contract.

notes: P0.2 concise repo-wide AGENTS projection.

artifacts.title: Control-plane module AGENTS projection

artifacts.title: Packet brief schema

dependency_refs.notes: The packet brief schema is derived from the accepted P0.2 operating contract.

artifacts.title: Factory Operating Contract

dependency_refs.notes: P0.3 scope, deliverables, and acceptance rules come from the master plan.
P0.3 extends the accepted P0.2 operating contract rather than redefining it.
Headless and packet execution rules build on the accepted packet-brief baseline.
P0.3 reuses accepted artifact, dependency, validation-hook, and stale-rule semantics.
The contract preserves the accepted three-layer architecture.
The contract preserves the fixed release doctrine and forbidden shortcuts.
The contract preserves human-owned product truth and the north-star direction.

notes: P0.3 normative Factory-specific execution contract.
Defines project skills, headless execution conventions, and automated review/approval rules without reopening the accepted P0.2 baseline.

artifacts.title: Factory workspace AGENTS projection

dependency_refs.notes: The .factory subtree narrows the repo-wide AGENTS projection for Factory assets.

notes: P0.3 descendant AGENTS projection for project-scoped Factory assets.

artifacts.title: Control-plane packet execution skill

dependency_refs.notes: The skill operationalizes the accepted P0.3 Factory contract for bounded packet work.

The skill reuses accepted P0.1 artifact, dependency, validation-hook, and stale semantics.

notes: P0.3 shared skill for executing bounded control-plane packets in this repo.

artifacts.title: Review guidelines skill

dependency_refs.notes: The skill operationalizes the accepted P0.3 review and approval rules.

notes: P0.3 background review skill for automated review and approval-prep runs.
Focuses on baseline preservation, control-plane integrity, scope discipline, and human-owned acceptance boundaries.

The register formalizes the accepted forbidden shortcuts from the chat-native release doctrine.
The register preserves the accepted Task Studio handoff target and commissioned-work direction.
